author: @spehropefhany
text: Duplicating the behavior of Cermark (a material that allows a wimpy laser to mark metals) at substantially less cost would be on my wish list.

Peace.

author: @JohnTarbox
text: Some on the internet have suggested that these lasers can lose power rather quickly after 10-20 hours of use if you run them at full power. I am interested in learning if you encounter this issue or not?

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: I Haven't encountered this issue yet, but I have only run the engraver at 100% once. It was too powerful for what I was doing. Thanks mate! :)

author: @Nuts-Bolts
text: Workshop? Oh come on.  Semantically, I would have thought ‘Playground’ is a more appropriate descriptive  noun.   ;¬ )

replies.author: @Nuts-Bolts
replies.text:
@hanslepoeter5167Verily. “That which we call rosin flux, and cutting fluid,
By any other names would smell as sweet.”

author: @mikegLXIVMM
text:
An idea I wanted try try:
Add Ammonium Buifluoride powder to black paint. Grind it in thoroughly with a paint muller or similar..
 Paint a glass surface with it.
Laser engrave the painted glass.
What I think will happen is, the Ammonium Bifluoride in the paint will vaporize where it has been in contact with the laser and in turn, etch the glass.

replies.author: @jamescunliffe9872
replies.text: I was going to suggest something similar but decided to read first! I wouldn't want to handle biflouride though!

replies.author: @mikegLXIVMM
@jamescunliffe9872
replies.text: @mikegLXIVMM yes I imagine it would be a calcified pineal gland hazard! ;)

author: @Arachnoid_of_the_underverse
text: Looking good Luke, if you make another maybe change the letters with objects i.e. swap the Os with a nut design and the T with a hammer.

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: That would be awesome! What a great idea, I might have to do that! Cheers mate! :)

author: @serfer4219
text: just wondering if you can cut thin steel like a coffee can with this laser, have been cutting the transformer lamination's with scissors, lol not as nice as laser cut

replies.author: @MyNameIsLukey
replies.text: To be honest I don't know. But it would be interesting to find out. Thanks mate! :)

author: @zaprodk
text: You may want to look into getting some safety glasses that are usable for the blue laser. Those green glasses do not offer any protection unfortunately.
